title: [BUG] Negative values format in Apple's Numbers
author: naorpeled
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

When using the following format ```_("$"* #,##0.00_);_("$"* -#,##0.00_);_("$"* 0_);_(@_)```
negative numbers on Excel are shown properly, but on Apple's Numbers it's shown as double negative.
For example:
Excel: -6
Numbers: --6

Lib version: 4.2.0

## Steps To Reproduce

<!-- The exact steps required to reproduce the issue, ideally with a code example -->

```javascript
const wb = new ExcelJS.Workbook();
const ws = wb.addWorksheet('XYZ');

ws.getCell('A1').value = -6;
ws.getCell('A1').numFmt = '_("$"* #,##0.00_);_("$"* -#,##0.00_);_("$"* 0_);_(@_)';

expect(ws.getCell('A1').value).to.equal(-6);
```

## The expected behaviour:
Negative numbers should look the same on both Excel and Numbers.
